zh.lifeSummary: 當Captain John William Routson於1796年2月12日出生在美國賓夕法尼亞州亞當斯格曼 堂史普時，他的父親Johannes John Routson27歲，母親Anna Maria Kufer29歲。他於1816年2月28日與Sarah Franks結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1834年6月17日在美國俄亥俄州韋恩享年38歲，埋葬在美國俄亥俄州諾布爾素莫費爾德易亞斯特 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Routson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: The progenitor of the Routsons (and Routzahns) was Johann Ludwig Rauenzahner from Germany, who in the middle of the 18th century settled in PA.

variants.name: Routzahn
meanings: Americanized form of German Rauenzahner: probably a nickname for someone with very irregular or ugly teeth, from Middle High German rouch, rūch ‘rough, hairy, unkempt’ + zant ‘tooth’ + -er agent suffix. The surname Rauenzahner has died out in Germany. Compare Routson .
History: The progenitor of the Routzahns (and Routsons) was Johann Ludwig Rauenzahner from Germany, who in the middle of the 18th century settled in PA.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
Ohio was the first state admitted to the Union from the Northwest Territory.

historicEvents.title: Change of capital city
historicEvents.description: Zanesville becomes the new state capital.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: Making States Equal
historicEvents.description: The Missouri Compromise helped provide the entrance of Maine as a free state and Missouri as a slave state into the United States. As part of the compromise, slavery was prohibited north of the 36°30′ parallel, excluding Missouri.

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.

historicEvents.title: The Oregon Trail
historicEvents.description: Many people started their 2,170-mile West trek to settle the land found by Louis and Clark. They used large-wheeled wagons to pack most of their belongings and were guided by trails that were made by the previous trappers and traders who walked the area. Over time the trail needed annual improvements to make the trip faster and safer. Most of Interstate 80 and 84 cover most of the ground that was the original trail.

en.lifeSummary: When Captain John William Routson was born on 12 February 1796, in Germany Township, Adams, Pennsylvania, United States, his father, Johannes John Routson, was 27 and his mother, Anna Maria Kufer, was 29. He married Sarah Franks on 28 February 1816. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 17 June 1834, in Wayne, Ohio, United States, at the age of 38, and was buried in Eastern Cemetery, Summerfield, Noble, Ohio, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Captain John William Routson est né le 12 février 1796, à Germany Township, Adams, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis, son père, Johannes John Routson, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Anna Maria Kufer, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Sarah Franks le 28 février 1816. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 17 juin 1834, à Wayne, Ohio, États-Unis, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterré à Eastern Cemetery, Summerfield, Noble, Ohio, États-Unis.

ko.lifeSummary: Captain John William Routson(이)가 1796년 2월 12일 미국 펜실베이니아 주 애댐스 그먼이 타운시프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Johannes John Routson(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Anna Maria Kufer(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1816년 2월 28일 Sarah Franks(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1834년 6월 17일 미국 오하이오 주 웨이인에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 오하이오 주 노블일 수머필드 이스터 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Captain John William Routson nasceu em 12 de fevereiro de 1796, em Germany Township, Adams, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Johannes John Routson, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Anna Maria Kufer, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah Franks em 28 de fevereiro de 1816. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 17 de junho de 1834, em Wayne, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América, com 38 anos, e foi sepultado em Eastern Cemetery, Summerfield, Noble, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Captain John William Routson родился 12 февраля 1796 г. в местности джирмани тоуншип, адамс, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Johannes John Routson было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Anna Maria Kufer, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Franks 28 февраля 1816 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 17 июня 1834 г. в местности веин, Огайо, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 38 лет и был похоронен в местности эастирн кеметири, суммирфиелд, нобл, Огайо, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Captain John William Routson nació el 12 de febrero de 1796, en Germany Township, Adams, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos, su papá, Johannes John Routson, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Anna Maria Kufer, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Sarah Franks el 28 de febrero de 1816. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 17 de junio de 1834, en Wayne, Ohio, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultado en Eastern Cemetery, Summerfield, Noble, Ohio, Estados Unidos.
